title: Jefferson's Document Copier
description: This desk technology provides a glimpse into the mechanical ingenuity of the 18th century, and Jefferson's proclivity for writing. This device allowed for the simultaneous duplication of letters and pre-dates the polygraph.
creator: Non-Applicable
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Mountain View at Monticello
description: A scenic look at the surrounding landscape of the estate, more specifically at the end of the front entraceway's walkway. This look demonstrates the natural beauty of the Virginia Piedmont that Jefferson carefully integrated into his home's designl.
creator: Non-Applicable
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Monticello Garden
description: This view showcases the cultivated grounds where Jefferson experimented with hundreds of varieties of vegetables and ornamental plants throughout his life. In modernity, the estate still uses the grounds to produce goods such as honey and honey byproducts.
creator: Non-Applicable
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Patsy Jefferson's Desk
description: Dating back to the 1780s, this personal furniture belonged to Marth "Patsy" Jefferson, Jefferson's daughter who served as his First Lady, and highlights the private domestic life of the Jefferson family.
creator: Unknown
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Jefferson Children's Playroom
description: Located in the uppermost room of the house, this space provides a unique look at the spaces designed for the younger generation of the family during the 1770s. The uppermost room did not have great ventilation, so it was often unused by Jefferson and instead the children would use the space to play; later owners of the home would similarly use the space, with one family putting a billiards table in place!
creator: Jefferson, Thomas
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Monticello Window
description: Featuring an intricate pulley system, the windows lining the top floor of the estate are a testament to Jefferson's fascination with architectural innovation and natural light.
creator: Jefferson, Thomas
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Mr. Madison's Room at Monticello
description: This guest suite, dating to the 1810s, highlights the lifelong friendship between Jefferson and James Madison, who was a frequent visitor to the estate since his own home of Montpelier was just 30 miles away.
creator: Jefferson, Thomas
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Monticello Skylight
description: An architectural feature from the original construction of the estate that demonstrates Jefferson's early use of overhead light to illuminate the interior spaces of his neoclassical mansion.
creator: Jefferson, Thomas
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Jefferson's Personal Study Desk
description: A functional piece of history, this desk served as the station for Jefferson's extensive correspondance and intellectual pursuits. The attached cabinet is full of personal effects, from books to letters and small art pieces.
creator: Unknown
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Monticello Great Clock
description: Designed by Peter Spunk in 1804, these clock weights are a part of the elaborate dual-faced timepiece that dominates the entrance hall of the home and a second face above the exterior front door. There was a slight miscalculation that resulted in holes needing to be cut into the floor to properly accomodate the ball weights.
creator: Spruck, Peter
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Madison's Personal Study Desk
description: This desk was a central fixture in the upper-level room where Madison drafted influential political documents from American history. From his desk, Madison had a sprawling view of his grounds.
creator: Unknown
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Madison's Study Literature
description: A close-up of specific volumes from James Madison’s personal library; these books were foundational to his intellectual life outside of his political career.
creator: Non-Applicable
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Montpelier Drawing Room
description: This space served as the social heart of the Madison home; Dolley Madison was known for inviting close family and friends in often, and hosted both informal and formal gatherings frequently.
creator: Madison Sr., James
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Montpelier Gilded Clock
description: Dolley Madison was ornate in her decor decisions, and this elegant decorative piece reflects the refined domestic style she brought to the estate during her time as hostess.
creator: Unknown
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: View at Montpelier
description: A view from the windows just behind the front porch, offering a sweeping look at the ground that Madison managed after his retirement from public life.
creator: Non-Applicable
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Slavery Exhibit at Monpelier
description: This modern exhibit provides essential historical context regarding the enslaved community that lived and workds on the Madison Plantation. The exhibit extends the entire basement area, with other stations of personal artifacts like the ones shown here.
creator: Chew, Elizabeth
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Exterior Windows at Montpelier
description: These architectural feature date back to the original construction by James Madison Sr. and define the historic and stately facade of the manor.
creator: Madison Sr., James
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.

title: Montpelier "Temple" Gazebo
description: Built in 1810 to cover an excavated ice pit, this structure shows clear architectural design influence from Jefferson and stands as a key feature of the north yard.
creator: Madison, James
rights: Allowed for non-commercial distribution and educational purposes only.
